_meta.note: Daemon-backed explicit reposition of one subscription's offset for one bucket (Phase 3). The requested seq is CLAMPED to the bucket's live range [head_seq-1, tail_seq] and is NEVER an error: a seek above tail parks at tail; a seek below the surviving head (head_seq-1) parks at the floor and sets lagged=true (the requested events were already evicted). The write is scope-guarded: an offset is stored ONLY for a bucket the subscription actually routes to (in predicate scope, or already tracked); seeking an out-of-scope bucket still reports the clamp but leaves no dangling offset. Repositioning a bucket the daemon does not know returns bucket_not_found; an unknown/malformed sub_id returns unknown_subscription. Phase 3 adds NO new error code.

response_example_clamped_lagged._note: AC12 eviction clamp: with max_events=3 and 5 appends, FIFO evicts seq 1,2 so head_seq=3 and the seek floor is head_seq-1=2. A seek to seq 0 (below the surviving head) clamps to the floor and reports lagged=true. This is a successful response, not an error -- the consumer is simply told it lagged behind eviction and where it was parked.

unknown_subscription_error_example._note: An unknown or malformed sub_id (unknown handle, or reset by a daemon restart) surfaces as the unknown_subscription IPC code. The MCP facade maps every caller-fixable IPC error to JSON-RPC invalid_params (-32602) so the agent corrects its input and keeps routing through Terminal Commander; data.ipc_code carries the typed daemon code verbatim. The remedy is to re-open the subscription.

invariants: sub_id, bucket_id, and seq are all required
the requested seq is clamped to the bucket's live range [head_seq-1, tail_seq] and is NEVER an error
a within-range seek is a no-op clamp: clamped_seq equals the requested seq and lagged is false
a seek below the surviving head (head_seq-1) clamps to the floor and reports lagged=true
a seek above tail_seq clamps down to tail_seq
the offset write is scope-guarded: only an in-scope or already-tracked bucket gets a stored offset; seeking an out-of-scope bucket reports the clamp but creates no dangling offset
an unknown or malformed sub_id returns the unknown_subscription IPC code (mapped to invalid_params on the wire); the caller re-opens
a bucket the daemon does not know returns bucket_not_found
Phase 3 adds NO new error code; clamp + lagged carry the eviction semantics on a successful response
